---
title: "Feature Engineering"
author: "Yaxing Li"
date: "2025-03-05"
output: html_document
---
# Import the data

```{r}
library(tidyverse) 
data <- read_csv("../01. data wrangling/data_wrangled.csv.gz")
```
# Accelerometer
### Accelerometer feature engineering
```{r, cache=TRUE}

data_accelerometer_wide <- data |>   # isolate accelerometer signals 
  filter(sensor == "accelerometer") |> 

  # we'll want a wide format to make the mutation easier
  pivot_wider(names_from = "signal", values_from = "value")

accelerometer_data <- data |> 
  # isolate accelerometer signals 
  filter(sensor == "accelerometer") |> 

  # we'll want a wide format to make the mutation easier
  pivot_wider(names_from = "signal", values_from = "value") |>   # add cartesian distance for accelerometer signals
  mutate(magnitude = sqrt(x ^ 2 + y ^ 2 + z ^ 2)) |>  group_by(participant_id, chunk_id) |>  # Group by time window
    summarize(
        mean_mag = mean(magnitude),
        sd_mag = sd(magnitude),
        max_mag = max(magnitude),
        min_mag = min(magnitude),
        mad_mag = mad(magnitude),
        .groups = "drop"
    )
summary(accelerometer_data)

#check any outliers in max_mag
ggplot(accelerometer_data, aes(y = max_mag)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of Max Acceleration Magnitude")

# identify these outliers in max_mag
## Compute Q1 (25th percentile) and Q3 (75th percentile)
Q1 <- quantile(accelerometer_data$max_mag, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
Q3 <- quantile(accelerometer_data$max_mag, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
IQR_value <- Q3 - Q1  # Compute the Interquartile Range (IQR)

## Define lower and upper bounds
lower_bound <- Q1 - 1.5 * IQR_value
upper_bound <- Q3 + 1.5 * IQR_value

outliers <- accelerometer_data |> 
  filter(max_mag < lower_bound | max_mag > upper_bound)

## Display the outlier rows
print(outliers)

## Display the outlier of participant_id == 1 plot
data |> 
  # isolate accelerometer signals 
  filter(sensor == "accelerometer") |> 

  # we'll want a wide format to make the mutation easier
  pivot_wider(names_from = "signal", values_from = "value") |>   # add cartesian distance for accelerometer signals 
  filter(participant_id == 1, chunk_id == 29) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(x, y, z), names_to = "axis", values_to = "acceleration") |>  
  ggplot(aes(x = unix_timestamp, y = acceleration, color = axis)) + 
  geom_line() + 
  facet_grid(axis ~ .) +  # Facet the plot by x, y, z axes
  labs(title = "Accelerometer Signals (X, Y, Z) Over Time", 
       x = "Timestamp", y = "Acceleration") +
  theme_minimal()

### remoove outliers (max_mag>5) in participant_id == 1
# Store the rows where z has its maximum value for each chunk as a tibble
outliers_tibble <- data |> 
  # Isolate accelerometer signals 
  filter(sensor == "accelerometer") |> 

  # Convert to wide format
  pivot_wider(names_from = "signal", values_from = "value") |>  

  # Filter for specific participant and chunk
  filter(participant_id == 1, chunk_id %in% c(29)) |>  

  # Select the row where "z" has its maximum value (for each chunk separately)
  group_by(chunk_id) |>  
  filter(z == max(z, na.rm = TRUE)) |>  
  ungroup()

# View the tibble of outliers
outliers_tibble


## Display the outlier of participant_id == 5 plot
data |> 
  # isolate accelerometer signals 
  filter(sensor == "accelerometer") |> 

  # we'll want a wide format to make the mutation easier
  pivot_wider(names_from = "signal", values_from = "value") |>   # add cartesian distance for accelerometer signals 
  filter(participant_id == 5, chunk_id == 11) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(x, y, z), names_to = "axis", values_to = "acceleration") |>  
  ggplot(aes(x = unix_timestamp, y = acceleration, color = axis)) + 
  geom_line() + 
  facet_grid(axis ~ .) +  # Facet the plot by x, y, z axes
  labs(title = "Accelerometer Signals (X, Y, Z) Over Time", 
       x = "Timestamp", y = "Acceleration") +
  theme_minimal()

### identify outliers in participant_id == 5
# Add the new rows where z has its maximum value for participant 5 and chunk 11 to the existing outliers_tibble
outliers_tibble <- outliers_tibble |> 
  bind_rows(
    data |> 
      # Isolate accelerometer signals 
      filter(sensor == "accelerometer") |> 

      # Convert to wide format
      pivot_wider(names_from = "signal", values_from = "value") |>  

      # Filter for specific participant and chunk
      filter(participant_id == 5, chunk_id %in% c(11)) |>  

      # Select the row where "z" has its maximum value (for each chunk separately)
      group_by(chunk_id) |>  
      filter(z == max(z, na.rm = TRUE)) |>  
      ungroup()
  )

# View the updated tibble
outliers_tibble

## Display the outlier of participant_id == 6 plot
data |> 
  # isolate accelerometer signals 
  filter(sensor == "accelerometer") |> 

  # we'll want a wide format to make the mutation easier
  pivot_wider(names_from = "signal", values_from = "value") |>   # add cartesian distance for accelerometer signals 
  filter(participant_id == 6, chunk_id == 57) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(x, y, z), names_to = "axis", values_to = "acceleration") |>  
  ggplot(aes(x = unix_timestamp, y = acceleration, color = axis)) + 
  geom_line() + 
  facet_grid(axis ~ .) +  # Facet the plot by x, y, z axes
  labs(title = "Accelerometer Signals (X, Y, Z) Over Time", 
       x = "Timestamp", y = "Acceleration") +
  theme_minimal()

### remove outliers(max_mag>5) in participant_id == 6
# Add the new rows where z has its maximum value for participant 5 and chunk 11 to the existing outliers_tibble
outliers_tibble <- outliers_tibble |> 
  bind_rows(
    data |> 
      # Isolate accelerometer signals 
      filter(sensor == "accelerometer") |> 

      # Convert to wide format
      pivot_wider(names_from = "signal", values_from = "value") |>  

      # Filter for specific participant and chunk
      filter(participant_id == 6, chunk_id %in% c(32,37,54,57)) |>  

      # Select the row where "z" has its maximum value (for each chunk separately)
      group_by(chunk_id) |>  
      filter(z == max(z, na.rm = TRUE)) |>  
      ungroup()
  )

# View the updated tibble
outliers_tibble

# Remove rows from data where unix_timestamp is within 1 second of any unix_timestamp in outliers_tibble
data_max_mag_cleaned <- data_accelerometer_wide %>%
  rowwise() %>%  # Process each row of data individually
  filter(
    !any(
      outliers_tibble$participant_id == participant_id &
      outliers_tibble$chunk_id == chunk_id &
      abs(outliers_tibble$unix_timestamp - unix_timestamp) <= 1
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()  # Ungroup the data after rowwise processing

# View the cleaned data
data_max_mag_cleaned
data_max_mag_cleaned_long <- data_max_mag_cleaned %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(x, y, z), names_to = "signal", values_to = "value")


# max_mag_cleaned data
accelerometer_data_max_mag_cleaned <- data_max_mag_cleaned_long |> 
  # isolate accelerometer signals 
  filter(sensor == "accelerometer") |> 

  # we'll want a wide format to make the mutation easier
  pivot_wider(names_from = "signal", values_from = "value") |>   # add cartesian distance for accelerometer signals
  mutate(magnitude = sqrt(x ^ 2 + y ^ 2 + z ^ 2)) |>  group_by(participant_id, chunk_id) |>  # Group by time window
    summarize(
        mean_mag = mean(magnitude),
        sd_mag = sd(magnitude),
        max_mag = max(magnitude),
        min_mag = min(magnitude),
        mad_mag = mad(magnitude),
        .groups = "drop"
    )
summary(accelerometer_data_max_mag_cleaned)

#check any outliers in max_mag
ggplot(accelerometer_data_max_mag_cleaned, aes(y = max_mag)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of Max Acceleration Magnitude Cleaned")
```

# BVP (heart rate)
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(e1071)  # For skewness and kurtosis

# Extract BVP signals
data_bvp <- data |> 
  filter(sensor == "bvp")

# Calculate time differences (d_time) and BPM for BVP signals
feature_bpm <- data_bvp |> 
  group_by(participant_id, chunk_id) |> 
  # Get local maxima (detect peaks)
  filter(value > lag(value), value > lead(value)) |> 
  # Calculate time difference between consecutive beats (d_time)
  mutate(d_time = unix_timestamp - lag(unix_timestamp, default = 0)) |> 
  # Filter out likely duplicates (time difference less than 0.25)
  filter(d_time >= 0.25) |> 
  # Calculate beats per minute (bpm)
  summarize(bpm = (60 / mean(d_time, na.rm = TRUE)), 
            d_time = mean(d_time, na.rm = TRUE), # also calculate average d_time for chunk
            .groups = "drop") |> 
  # Filter out bpm values less than 50
  filter(bpm >= 50)

# Check the lowest bpm values after filtering
feature_bpm |> 
  arrange(bpm) |> 
  head()


# Calculate BVP features including HRV (Standard Deviation of RR intervals)
bvp_features <- data_bvp %>%
  group_by(participant_id, chunk_id) %>%
  summarize(
    mean_bvp = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_bvp = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_bvp = min(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_bvp = max(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    skewness_bvp = skewness(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    kurtosis_bvp = kurtosis(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

bvp_features

# Join bvp_features with feature_bpm to include bpm and d_time
final_bvp_features <- left_join( feature_bpm,bvp_features, by = c("participant_id", "chunk_id"))

# Display the final features
final_bvp_features
```

# eda
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(e1071)  # For skewness and kurtosis
library(pracma) # For peak detection
# Extract EDA signals
data_eda <- data |> filter(sensor == "eda")

# Extract EDA signals for Participant 1, Chunk 1
eda_data <- data %>%
  filter(sensor == "eda", participant_id == 8, chunk_id == 7) %>%
  arrange(unix_timestamp)  # Ensure sorted by time

# Detect peaks in EDA
eda_peaks <- eda_data %>%
  mutate(
    d_time = unix_timestamp - lag(unix_timestamp),  # Compute time difference
    diff1 = value - lag(value),  # First derivative
    diff2 = lead(value) - value  # Next step derivative
  ) %>%
  filter(diff1 > 0 & diff2 < 0 & value > 0.05)  # Identify peaks (SCRs)

# Plot the EDA signal with detected peaks
ggplot(eda_data, aes(x = unix_timestamp, y = value)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +  # EDA signal line
  geom_point(data = eda_peaks, aes(x = unix_timestamp, y = value), 
             color = "red", size = 3) +  # Highlight peaks in red
  labs(
    title = "EDA Signal with Detected Peaks (Participant 1, Chunk 1)",
    x = "Time (Unix Timestamp)",
    y = "EDA Value"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Detect peaks in EDA for phasic response detection
eda_phasic <- eda_data %>%
  mutate(
    d_time = unix_timestamp - lag(unix_timestamp),  # Compute time difference
    diff1 = value - lag(value),  # First derivative (rate of change)
    diff2 = lead(value) - value  # Next derivative
  ) %>%
  filter(diff1 > 0 & diff2 < 0 & value > 0.05)  # Local maxima

# Calculate amplitude and recovery time for phasic responses
eda_phasic <- eda_phasic %>%
  mutate(
    peak_amplitude = value - lag(value, default = first(value)),  # Amplitude from previous value
    recovery_time = lead(unix_timestamp) - unix_timestamp  # Recovery time to next peak
  )


# View the detected phasic responses
eda_phasic %>%
  select(participant_id, chunk_id, unix_timestamp, value, peak_amplitude, recovery_time) %>%
  head()

# Compute tonic features (SCL)
eda_tonic <- data_eda %>%
  group_by(participant_id, chunk_id) %>%
  summarize(
    mean_scl = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_scl = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_scl = min(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_scl = max(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    scl_slope = (last(value) - first(value)) / (n() - 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
eda_tonic

# Detect phasic responses (SCR)
all_chunks <- data_eda %>%
  select(participant_id, chunk_id) %>%
  distinct()

eda_phasic <- data_eda %>%
  group_by(participant_id, chunk_id) %>%
  mutate(
    d_time = unix_timestamp - lag(unix_timestamp),
    diff1 = value - lag(value),
    diff2 = lead(value) - value
  ) %>%
  filter(diff1 > 0 & diff2 < 0 & value > 0.05) %>%
  group_by(participant_id, chunk_id) %>%
  summarize(
    scr_peaks = n(),
    scr_mean_amplitude = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    scr_max_amplitude = max(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Left join to keep all chunks, even those with no peaks
eda_phasic <- left_join(all_chunks, eda_phasic, by = c("participant_id", "chunk_id"))

# Replace NA values with 0 for chunks with no detected peaks
eda_phasic <- eda_phasic %>%
  mutate(
    scr_peaks = ifelse(is.na(scr_peaks), 0, scr_peaks),
    scr_mean_amplitude = ifelse(is.na(scr_mean_amplitude), 0, scr_mean_amplitude),
    scr_max_amplitude = ifelse(is.na(scr_max_amplitude), 0, scr_max_amplitude)
  )

# View the result
eda_phasic


# Combine all features
eda_features <- left_join(eda_tonic, eda_phasic, by = c("participant_id", "chunk_id"))

# Display features
eda_features

# Reshape the data to long format
eda_features_long <- eda_features %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = mean_scl:scl_slope, names_to = "feature", values_to = "value")

# Create the boxplot for each feature
ggplot(eda_features_long, aes(x = feature, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of EDA Features for Each Row", x = "Feature", y = "Value") +
  theme_minimal()

# Calculate the IQR for the max_scl feature
Q1 <- quantile(eda_features$max_scl, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
Q3 <- quantile(eda_features$max_scl, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
IQR <- Q3 - Q1

# Define the outlier thresholds
lower_bound <- Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
upper_bound <- Q3 + 1.5 * IQR

# Identify rows with outliers in max_scl
outliers <- eda_features %>%
  filter(max_scl < lower_bound | max_scl > upper_bound)

# View outlier rows
outliers

# participant 8 max_scl>3 in break labels? delete?
eda_features_filtered_max_scl <- eda_features %>%
  filter(max_scl <= 2)

# View the filtered data
eda_features_filtered_max_scl
```

# temperature
```{R}

# Extract EDA signals
data_temperature <- data |> filter(sensor == "temperature")

# Assuming 'data_temperature' contains unix_timestamp and value columns
temperature_features <- data_temperature %>%
  group_by(participant_id, chunk_id) %>%
  mutate(
    # Calculate time difference (d_time) between consecutive time points
    d_time = unix_timestamp - lag(unix_timestamp),  
    # Calculate the rate of change (dT) between consecutive time points
    rate_of_change = (value - lag(value)) / d_time
  ) %>%
  summarize(
    mean_temp = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_temp = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_temp = min(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_temp = max(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    range_temp = max(value, na.rm = TRUE) - min(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_temp = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    skewness_temp = skewness(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    kurtosis_temp = kurtosis(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_rate_of_change = mean(rate_of_change, na.rm = TRUE),  # Mean rate of change
    max_rate_of_change = max(rate_of_change, na.rm = TRUE),    # Maximum rate of change
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# View the temperature features with rate of change
temperature_features

# Calculate IQR-based outliers for temperature features
temperature_features_outliers <- temperature_features %>%
  mutate(
    # Calculate IQR for each feature
    iqr_temp = IQR(c(min_temp, max_temp), na.rm = TRUE),
    lower_bound = quantile(c(min_temp, max_temp), 0.25, na.rm = TRUE) - 1.5 * iqr_temp,
    upper_bound = quantile(c(min_temp, max_temp), 0.75, na.rm = TRUE) + 1.5 * iqr_temp
  ) %>%
  filter(min_temp < lower_bound | max_temp > upper_bound)  # Filter out the outliers based on IQR


# View the outliers
temperature_features_outliers

# Create a boxplot for min_temp and max_temp, highlighting outliers
ggplot(temperature_features, aes(x = factor(1), y = min_temp)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.colour = "red", outlier.size = 3) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(y = max_temp), outlier.colour = "blue", outlier.size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of Temperature Features (min_temp and max_temp)",
       x = "Temperature Features",
       y = "Temperature Value") +
  theme_minimal()

# Filter the data for participant_id == 1 and chunk_id == 1
data_temperature_filtered <- data_temperature %>%
  filter(participant_id == 4, chunk_id == 12)

# Plot the temperature values
ggplot(data_temperature_filtered, aes(x = unix_timestamp, y = value)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Temperature over Time for Participant 1, Chunk 1",
       x = "Time (Unix Timestamp)",
       y = "Temperature Value") +
  theme_minimal()
```

# corrlelation between tempature and heart rate and scl
```{R}
library(corrplot)

merged_data <- temperature_features %>%
  left_join(eda_features_filtered_max_scl, by = c("participant_id", "chunk_id")) %>%
  left_join(final_bvp_features, by = c("participant_id", "chunk_id"))

# Calculate correlation matrix for selected features
cor_matrix <- merged_data %>%
  select(mean_temp, mean_rate_of_change, bpm, mean_scl,scr_peaks) %>%  # Adjust feature names as needed
  cor(use = "complete.obs", method = "pearson")  # Calculate Pearson correlation (you can change to 'spearman' if needed)

# View the correlation matrix
print(cor_matrix)

# Visualize the correlation matrix
corrplot(cor_matrix, method = "circle", type = "upper", tl.col = "black", diag = FALSE)

# You can also plot scatter plots to see relationships
ggplot(merged_data, aes(x = bpm, y = mean_temp)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Correlation between BPM and Temperature")

ggplot(merged_data, aes(x = bpm, y = scr_peaks)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Correlation between BPM and SCR Peaks")

# Fit a linear model
lm_model <- lm(bpm ~ scr_peaks, data = merged_data)

# Predict bpm based on scr_peaks and create a new variable
merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  mutate(
    bpm_predicted_from_scr_peaks = predict(lm_model, newdata = merged_data)
  )
```

# cook them together
```{R}
accelerometer_data_max_mag_cleaned
final_bvp_features
eda_features_filtered_max_scl
temperature_features
merged_data$bpm_predicted_from_scr_peaks
# Merge the datasets one by one, ensuring that missing observations are filled with NAs
merged_data <- temperature_features %>%
  full_join(eda_features_filtered_max_scl, by = c("participant_id", "chunk_id")) %>%
  full_join(accelerometer_data_max_mag_cleaned, by = c("participant_id", "chunk_id")) %>%
  full_join(final_bvp_features, by = c("participant_id", "chunk_id")) %>%
  full_join(merged_data %>% select(participant_id, chunk_id, bpm_predicted_from_scr_peaks), 
            by = c("participant_id", "chunk_id"))

# View the merged data
head(merged_data)


# Merge the labels into the merged_data
label_data_unique <- data %>%
  select(participant_id, chunk_id, label) %>%
  distinct()

final_label_merged_data <- merged_data %>%
  left_join(label_data_unique , by = c("participant_id", "chunk_id"))

split_data_unique <- data %>%
  select(participant_id, chunk_id, split) %>%
  distinct()

final_split_merged_data <- final_label_merged_data %>%
  left_join(split_data_unique , by = c("participant_id", "chunk_id"))

# Save the final engineered dataset to CSV
write.csv(final_split_merged_data, "final_engineered_data.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```
